policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding guidelines requiring banks with assets over ₹1 lakh crore to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP by March 2027. Banks must report physical risk exposures by sector and geography using standardized templates.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Include Scope 3 Emissions for Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI mandated Scope 3 emissions disclosure under BRSR Core for top 500 companies by market cap, effective FY2027. Reasonable assurance requirements extended to all climate metrics from limited assurance.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Framework for Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft regulations allowing parametric triggers based on IMD weather data, satellite indices, and government disaster declarations. Public comments invited until 30 April 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Requirements
policy.description: BCBS published final standard requiring banks to disclose climate-related financial risks under Pillar 3 from January 2028. Includes granular templates for physical risk by geography and transition risk by sector.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Pathways
policy.description: NGFS updated scenarios now include acute physical risk shocks at sub-national level and differentiated pathways for emerging markets. New 'Hot House World' variant models 3.5°C warming impacts on banking sector.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: European Commission Adopts Sector-Specific ESRS for Financial Institutions
policy.description: EC adopted final sector standards under CSRD requiring banks and insurers to disclose financed emissions, climate VaR, and physical risk exposure by asset class. Applies to EU subsidiaries of Indian banks from FY2027.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide climate risk scores for the bank's corporate lending portfolio across 50,000+ facilities in India. Deal reportedly valued at $4-5 million annually.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A for ESG Compliance Platform Expansion
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed Series A led by Accel India to expand its BRSR compliance automation platform. Company claims 80+ enterprise clients including three public sector banks.

competitors.title: XDI Launches Asset-Level Physical Risk API for Emerging Market Banks
competitors.description: XDI released a new API product offering physical risk scores at individual asset level for 15 emerging markets including India. Pricing starts at $50,000 annually for portfolio screening use cases.

competitors.title: Moody's Integrates India-Specific Physical Risk Scores into Credit Analytics
competitors.description: Moody's embedded district-level physical hazard data for India into its credit risk models, offering PD adjustments for flood, cyclone, and heat stress. Product targets Indian bank compliance with RBI guidelines.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Selected by SBI for Natural Capital Risk Assessment Pilot
competitors.description: GIST Advisory won mandate to conduct natural capital and biodiversity risk assessment for State Bank of India's agricultural lending portfolio covering ₹2.5 lakh crore in exposures.

competitors.title: Swiss Re and ICICI Lombard Launch Parametric Flood Cover for MSME Lenders
competitors.description: Swiss Re partnered with ICICI Lombard to offer parametric flood insurance embedded in MSME loans, using IMD rainfall data as trigger. Initial pilot covers 5 districts in Maharashtra and Gujarat.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Stress Test Methodology and Results
research.description: RBI's first climate stress test found that physical risks could increase NPAs by 1.8-4.2% under severe scenarios by 2030. Agricultural and MSME portfolios in eastern and coastal states show highest vulnerability.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Scaling Solutions in Emerging Markets
research.description: IFC report identifies India as the largest opportunity market for parametric insurance, projecting $2.1 billion in annual premium potential by 2030. Key barriers include basis risk perception and limited distribution through banks.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Challenges and Solutions for Financial Institutions
research.description: UNEPFI study finds that 67% of listed companies in India lack verified Scope 1 emissions data, rising to 89% for unlisted SMEs. Recommends satellite and proxy-based estimation as interim solutions for lender portfolios.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Lending: Evidence from South Asia
research.description: World Bank research shows banks in flood-prone Indian districts experience 23% higher loan defaults in years following major flood events. Study recommends integrating hazard data into credit underwriting processes.

research.title: Integrating Physical Risk into Credit Ratings: A Framework for Indian Corporates
research.description: CRISIL proposed methodology to adjust corporate credit ratings for physical climate risk, with potential 1-3 notch impacts for high-exposure sectors. Framework covers 22 sub-sectors across manufacturing and infrastructure.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate guidelines have started an arms race for India-specific physical risk data—StepChange must move fast before global players lock up bank relationships.
body: This month's signals converge on one theme: the compliance-driven climate risk market in Indian banking is now officially open. RBI's final guidelines (policy-1) create a hard deadline and specific data requirements, while parallel moves from Basel and NGFS ensure this isn't a one-off domestic push. The question is no longer whether banks will buy climate risk intelligence, but from whom.

The RBI stress test results (research-1) showing 1.8-4.2% NPA increases are the clearest validation of StepChange's core value proposition. Banks now have regulatory permission—indeed, obligation—to treat physical risk as a material credit factor. The specific call-out of agricultural and MSME portfolios in eastern and coastal states gives us a precise targeting map. Our PD/LGD models should prioritize these segments and geographies in sales collateral immediately.

The Jupiter-HDFC partnership (comp-1) is the most concerning competitor signal. A global player has landed a flagship Indian private bank with a multi-year, multi-million dollar deal. Meanwhile, Moody's is embedding India-specific physical risk into credit analytics (comp-4), and XDI is pricing aggressively for emerging market banks (comp-3). The window for StepChange to establish itself as the domestic alternative is narrowing. Our advantages—local regulatory alignment, INR pricing, and on-ground support—must be weaponized in every pitch.

The IFC parametric insurance report (research-2) projecting $2.1 billion in Indian premium potential validates our second strategic bet. The Swiss Re-ICICI Lombard pilot (comp-6) shows that bank-embedded distribution is the winning model. StepChange should accelerate conversations with NBFCs and regional banks who can't access Swiss Re-scale partnerships. The IRDAI draft framework (policy-3) gives us until April 30 to submit comments shaping the regulatory environment—we should participate actively.

Watch: SEBI's Scope 3 mandate (policy-2) will drive demand for supply chain and financed emissions data, where UNEPFI notes massive gaps (research-3). This could become our third revenue stream if we build proxy estimation capabilities. Action: Schedule meetings with HDFC Bank's competitors within 10 days—ICICI, Axis, Kotak—using RBI compliance deadlines as the urgency hook.
